title: Add LICHEN peer enumeration for Meshtastic node DB sync
description: Why this exists: project-LICHEN-t2hn.6 can emit the local self node_info, but Meshtastic want_config_id stage 69421 should include known peers once LICHEN has a peer/status table. What needs to be done: expose a bounded peer enumeration/status snapshot API with EUI/IID, name if known, last-heard, SNR/RSSI, hop distance, and optional metrics; wire Meshtastic node DB sync to emit one NodeInfo per peer. Acceptance criteria: enumeration is bounded and concurrency-safe; node-number collisions are deterministic and tracked; native_sim tests cover self-only and multi-peer output.
notes:
Implemented no-hardware Meshtastic NodeDB peer enumeration. Adapter now snapshots bounded peers through get_peers(), sorts deterministically by EUI-64, derives Meshtastic node numbers from low EUI-64 bits, emits self first, omits/tracks self/peer node-number collisions, preflights dynamic queue space for NodeDB/full sync, and sets MyInfo.nodedb_count to emitted self+peers. Gateway Meshtastic BLE now selects LICHEN_APP_IDENTITY and maps app_identity peers into peer snapshots. Added bounded peer name sanitization before strlen-based NodeInfo encoding. Production defaults constrained: NODEDB_MAX_PEERS default/range 3, gateway FromRadio queue range 20..32, adapter thread stack 4096.
Review evidence: explorer/test-design agents mapped existing app_identity snapshot APIs and Meshtastic adapter acceptance tests before implementation. Code review pass 1 found P1/P2 issues: production app_identity not selected, max-peer stack growth, unsafe non-NUL peer names, full-sync default queue capacity. Fixed by selecting app_identity, bounding peer cap to 3, raising gateway adapter/test stacks, sanitizing peer names, and constraining queue depth. Final review passes (Descartes/Pascal/Faraday/Gauss/Chandrasekhar) found no P0/P1/P2 blockers. P3 follow-ups filed: project-LICHEN-0e37 metric exposure policy, project-LICHEN-p2pu drift-proof Kconfig-derived queue regression, project-LICHEN-xehk qemu_x86 gateway harness, project-LICHEN-0awc physical BLE NodeDB validation.
Validation evidence: local git diff --check PASS; local python/.venv/bin/python -m pytest python/tests/test_vectors.py -q PASS (81 passed). EC2 us-east-2a clean worktree /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-t2hn65-validate with EBS vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58: native_sim Twister for app_identity, meshtastic_adapter, meshtastic_gateway_adapter PASS 3/3 configurations, 86 test cases, 0 warnings. qemu_x86 Twister for meshtastic_adapter PASS 1/1 configuration, 62 test cases, 0 warnings. native_sim gateway app build PASS; size build/gateway-native-t2hn64-final5/zephyr/zephyr.elf: text=150479 data=6001 bss=47933 dec=204413. qemu_x86 meshtastic_gateway_adapter was not kept in scope because qemu exits before Zephyr boot without /tmp/slip.sock: qemu-system-i386 -serial unix:/tmp/slip.sock failed with 'No such file or directory'; follow-up project-LICHEN-xehk filed and testcase constrained to native_sim with comment.
close_reason: Completed no-hardware scope: bounded deterministic Meshtastic NodeDB peer enumeration implemented, gateway app_identity peer source wired in production, native_sim/qemu/shared adapter validation passed, native gateway app build passed, reviews resolved. Follow-ups filed for metric exposure policy, drift-proof queue tests, qemu gateway harness, and physical BLE validation.
